name: Zayd ibn Arqam
title: Protesting Companion
shortDescription: The old companion of the Prophet (PBUH) who strongly protested against Ibn Ziyad's disrespect to Imam Hussain's (AS) holy head in his court.
fullStory:
Zayd ibn Arqam was a famous companion of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) who lived in Kufa at the time of the Ashura event and was an elderly man.

After the event of Karbala, when the caravan of captives and the holy heads of the martyrs were brought to Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad's palace (Dar al-Imara of Kufa), Zayd ibn Arqam was also present in that gathering.

Ibn Ziyad, out of malice and arrogance, started striking the lips and teeth of Imam Hussain's (AS) holy head with a cane he held (a bamboo stick). Unable to bear this scene, Zayd ibn Arqam shouted with tears and anger: 'Remove your cane from these lips and teeth!'

He continued: 'By God, I saw many times the Messenger of God (PBUH) kissing these lips and teeth.' Ibn Ziyad angrily said: 'If you hadn't grown old and senile, I would have beheaded you!' Zayd left the gathering crying while loudly cursing the killers of Hussain.
quote: (His shout at Ibn Ziyad:) Remove your cane from these lips! By God, I saw many times the Prophet kissing these lips.
